title: sambucus racemosa var. plumosa
intro: Sambucus racemosa var. plumosa, also known as the plume elder or red-berried elder, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and sambunigrin. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa var. plumosa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Sambucus racemosa var. plumosa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make medicinal remedies, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal products.
